memory.summary: I have a free afternoon with low stress, but my term project is critically behind schedule. While leisure and chores are tempting options, I must prioritize starting the project immediately.
Even a brief 30-minute break yielded minimal project progress, confirming that immediate, focused work is still the only way to address my critical deadline.
Spending 30 minutes on a trivial chore only advanced my project by 4%, confirming that leisure activities are not helping me catch up on my critically behind schedule term work.
Spending time on trivial tasks yields minimal progress while my project remains critically behind schedule as the afternoon slips away.
My recent work confirms that focused effort on the term project is essential, as procrastination only increases future stress and reduces my ability to avoid a crunch.
Spending 0.5 hours on trivial tasks only moved my progress by 3%, confirming that low-effort activities are ineffective while I remain critically behind schedule.
Spending another 0.5 hours on trivial tasks confirms that low-effort activities yield negligible progress while my project remains critically behind schedule.
Spending 0.5 hours on trivial tasks only moved my progress by 3%, confirming that low-effort activities are ineffective while I remain critically behind schedule.
Three hours of trivial work yielded only 0.03 total progress, confirming that low-effort activities are completely ineffective while I remain critically behind schedule.
Three hours on trivial tasks only yielded 0.03 progress, confirming these activities are ineffective while I remain critically behind schedule.
Spending time on trivial tasks is consistently ineffective, yielding negligible progress while I remain critically behind schedule.
Even after three hours, trivial work only added 0.03 progress, confirming these tasks are completely ineffective while I remain critically behind schedule.

goals.rationale: Progress is only at ~18% with a deadline in 3 days; dedicated focus is required now to prevent last-minute pressure, though it can be deferred slightly longer than the instant chore.

agent.last_reasoning: Finishing the trivial chore (g1) clears a high-priority obligation before the 22:00 deadline, preventing stress buildup. This allows me to then focus on the term project (g2) with a clear mind.
